en: I come to you with a message of leave-taking and farewell. This speech did not get very much attention.
ko: 이별과 작별의 메시지를 가지고 왔습니다. 이 연설은 많은 주목을 받지 못했습니다.
ja: 別れと告別のメッセージを持って参りました。この演説はあまり注目されませんでした。
zhTW: 我帶著離別和告別的訊息來到你們面前。這篇演說並沒有得到太多關注。
vi: Toi den voi cac ban voi thong diep chia tay va tu biet. Bai dien van nay khong nhan duoc nhieu su chu y.

en: When a new president is coming to power, as John Kennedy was, the spotlight was not on Dwight Eisenhower.
ko: 존 케네디 같은 신임 대통령이 취임할 때, 스포트라이트는 아이젠하워에게 있지 않았습니다.
ja: ジョン・ケネディのように新大統領が就任する時、スポットライトはアイゼンハワーに当たっていなかった。
zhTW: 當新總統即將上任，就像約翰·甘迺迪那樣，聚光燈並不在艾森豪身上。
vi: Khi mot tong thong moi len nam quyen, nhu John Kennedy, su chu y khong dat vao Dwight Eisenhower.

en: We have been compelled to create a permanent armaments industry of vast proportions.
ko: 우리는 거대한 규모의 영구적 군수 산업을 만들 수밖에 없었습니다.
ja: 我々は巨大な規模の恒久的な軍需産業を作らざるを得なかった。
zhTW: 我們被迫建立了一個規模龐大的永久軍備工業。
vi: Chung ta buoc phai tao ra mot nganh cong nghiep vu khi thuong truc voi quy mo khong lo.

en: There was a feeling at the time that this must have been written by some speechwriter who just sneaked it into the speech.
ko: 당시 이건 연설문 작성자가 몰래 넣은 것이라는 느낌이 있었습니다.
ja: 当時、これはどこかのスピーチライターがこっそり演説に忍び込ませたに違いないという感覚があった。
zhTW: 當時有種感覺，這一定是某個撰稿人偷偷塞進演說裡的。
vi: Luc do co cam giac rang day han la do nguoi viet dien van nao do len lut dua vao.

en: In the councils of government, we must guard against the acquisition of unwarranted influence, whether sought or unsought, by the military-industrial complex.
ko: 정부 회의에서 군산복합체의 부당한 영향력 획득을 경계해야 합니다.
ja: 政府の会議において、軍産複合体による不当な影響力の獲得を、意図的であれ偶発的であれ、警戒しなければならない。
zhTW: 在政府的決策中，我們必須防範軍工複合體獲取不當的影響力，無論是主動還是被動的。
vi: Trong cac hoi dong chinh phu, chung ta phai canh giac truoc viec toan bo quan su-cong nghiep gianh duoc anh huong khong chinh dang, du co chu y hay khong.

en: Three months ago, we got contacted by a family up in Minnesota saying that we have documents from Malcolm Moose.
ko: 3개월 전 미네소타의 한 가족이 말콤 무스의 문서가 있다고 연락해왔습니다.
ja: 3ヶ月前、ミネソタの家族から連絡があり、マルコム・ムースの文書があると言われた。
zhTW: 三個月前，明尼蘇達的一個家庭聯繫我們，說他們有馬爾科姆·穆斯的文件。
vi: Ba thang truoc, mot gia dinh o Minnesota lien he voi chung toi noi rang ho co tai lieu cua Malcolm Moose.

en: He was responsible, in part, for drafting the military-industrial complex speech.
ko: 그는 군산복합체 연설 초안 작성에 부분적으로 관여했습니다.
ja: 彼は軍産複合体演説の起草に部分的に関わっていた。
zhTW: 他部分負責起草了軍工複合體演說。
vi: Ong ta chiu trach nhiem mot phan trong viec soan thao bai dien van ve to hop quan su-cong nghiep.

en: These new papers give us written evidence that this was not just some caprice of Eisenhower's or something by some speechwriter.
ko: 이 새 문서는 이것이 아이젠하워의 변덕이나 연설문 작성자의 작품이 아니었다는 증거를 줍니다.
ja: これらの新しい文書は、これがアイゼンハワーの気まぐれやスピーチライターの作品ではなかったという書面の証拠を与えてくれる。
zhTW: 這些新文件給了我們書面證據，證明這不只是艾森豪的一時興起或某個撰稿人的作品。
vi: Nhung tai lieu moi nay cung cap bang chung bang van ban rang day khong chi la y thich nhat thoi cua Eisenhower hay tac pham cua nguoi viet dien van nao do.

en: You see the evolution of his speech from May 1959 to 1961.
ko: 1959년 5월부터 1961년까지 연설의 진화를 볼 수 있습니다.
ja: You see the evolution of his speech from May 1959 to 1961.
zhTW: You see the evolution of his speech from May 1959 to 1961.
vi: You see the evolution of his speech from May 1959 to 1961.

en: And he wanted to give this speech for a long time, two years.
ko: 그는 오래전부터, 2년간 이 연설을 하고 싶어 했습니다.
ja: And he wanted to give this speech for a long time, two years.
zhTW: And he wanted to give this speech for a long time, two years.
vi: And he wanted to give this speech for a long time, two years.

en: Our military organization today bears little relation to that known of any of my predecessors in peacetime, or indeed by the fighting men of World War II or Korea.
ko: 오늘날의 군사 조직은 평시의 전임 대통령들이나 2차 대전, 한국전쟁 참전 군인들이 알던 것과 거의 관련이 없습니다.
ja: Our military organization today bears little relation to that known of any of my predecessors in peacetime, or indeed by the fighting men of World War II or Korea.
zhTW: Our military organization today bears little relation to that known of any of my predecessors in peacetime, or indeed by the fighting men of World War II or Korea.
vi: Our military organization today bears little relation to that known of any of my predecessors in peacetime, or indeed by the fighting men of World War II or Korea.

en: There was one person in Dwight Eisenhower's life whom he really confided almost everything to, and that was his brother Milton.
ko: 아이젠하워가 거의 모든 것을 털어놓은 사람이 한 명 있었는데, 그의 형제 밀턴이었습니다.
ja: There was one person in Dwight Eisenhower's life whom he really confided almost everything to, and that was his brother Milton.
zhTW: There was one person in Dwight Eisenhower's life whom he really confided almost everything to, and that was his brother Milton.
vi: There was one person in Dwight Eisenhower's life whom he really confided almost everything to, and that was his brother Milton.

en: There's one particular document where the speechwriters had already drafted their version of his speech, only to see Milton come along and totally revamp what had already been written.
ko: 연설문 작성자들이 이미 초안을 만들었는데 밀턴이 와서 완전히 바꿔버린 문서가 있습니다.
ja: There's one particular document where the speechwriters had already drafted their version of his speech, only to see Milton come along and totally revamp what had already been written.
zhTW: There's one particular document where the speechwriters had already drafted their version of his speech, only to see Milton come along and totally revamp what had already been written.
vi: There's one particular document where the speechwriters had already drafted their version of his speech, only to see Milton come along and totally revamp what had already been written.

en: When Milton Eisenhower was taking notes and writing things on the drafts of these speeches, the speechwriters knew that it wasn't Milton talking, it was Ike.
ko: 밀턴이 초안에 메모를 할 때, 작성자들은 밀턴이 아니라 아이크가 말하는 거라는 걸 알았습니다.
ja: When Milton Eisenhower was taking notes and writing things on the drafts of these speeches, the speechwriters knew that it wasn't Milton talking, it was Ike.
zhTW: When Milton Eisenhower was taking notes and writing things on the drafts of these speeches, the speechwriters knew that it wasn't Milton talking, it was Ike.
vi: When Milton Eisenhower was taking notes and writing things on the drafts of these speeches, the speechwriters knew that it wasn't Milton talking, it was Ike.

en: The potential for the disastrous rise of misplaced power exists and will persist.
ko: 잘못된 권력의 재앙적 부상 가능성은 존재하며 지속될 것입니다.
ja: The potential for the disastrous rise of misplaced power exists and will persist.
zhTW: The potential for the disastrous rise of misplaced power exists and will persist.
vi: The potential for the disastrous rise of misplaced power exists and will persist.

en: He would see magazines with advertisements for some new war plane or some bomb, and he got so angry he'd take the magazine and throw it into the fireplace of the Oval Office because he felt that defense spending should not be something that would be encouraged by companies who are seeking commercial gain.
ko: 새 전투기나 폭탄 광고가 실린 잡지를 보면 화가 나서 대통령 집무실 벽난로에 던져버렸습니다. 국방비가 이익을 추구하는 기업에 의해 장려되어서는 안 된다고 생각했기 때문입니다.
ja: He would see magazines with advertisements for some new war plane or some bomb, and he got so angry he'd take the magazine and throw it into the fireplace of the Oval Office because he felt that defense spending should not be something that would be encouraged by companies who are seeking commercial gain.
zhTW: He would see magazines with advertisements for some new war plane or some bomb, and he got so angry he'd take the magazine and throw it into the fireplace of the Oval Office because he felt that defense spending should not be something that would be encouraged by companies who are seeking commercial gain.
vi: He would see magazines with advertisements for some new war plane or some bomb, and he got so angry he'd take the magazine and throw it into the fireplace of the Oval Office because he felt that defense spending should not be something that would be encouraged by companies who are seeking commercial gain.

en: We must never let the weight of this combination endanger our liberties or democratic processes.
ko: 이 결합의 무게가 우리의 자유와 민주적 과정을 위협하게 해서는 안 됩니다.
ja: We must never let the weight of this combination endanger our liberties or democratic processes.
zhTW: We must never let the weight of this combination endanger our liberties or democratic processes.
vi: We must never let the weight of this combination endanger our liberties or democratic processes.

en: There is an interesting document. It shows that the farewell speech would be made to Congress, but yet President Eisenhower decided no, he was going to address the people.
ko: 흥미로운 문서가 있습니다. 고별 연설을 의회에 하기로 했었지만, 아이젠하워는 국민에게 직접 말하기로 결정했습니다.
ja: There is an interesting document. It shows that the farewell speech would be made to Congress, but yet President Eisenhower decided no, he was going to address the people.
zhTW: There is an interesting document. It shows that the farewell speech would be made to Congress, but yet President Eisenhower decided no, he was going to address the people.
vi: There is an interesting document. It shows that the farewell speech would be made to Congress, but yet President Eisenhower decided no, he was going to address the people.

en: Only an alert and knowledgeable citizenry can compel the proper meshing of the huge industrial and military machinery of defense with our peaceful methods and goals, so that security and liberty may prosper together.
ko: 깨어 있고 지식 있는 시민만이 거대한 산업과 군사 기구를 평화적 방법과 목표에 맞게 조화시킬 수 있습니다.
ja: Only an alert and knowledgeable citizenry can compel the proper meshing of the huge industrial and military machinery of defense with our peaceful methods and goals, so that security and liberty may prosper together.
zhTW: Only an alert and knowledgeable citizenry can compel the proper meshing of the huge industrial and military machinery of defense with our peaceful methods and goals, so that security and liberty may prosper together.
vi: Only an alert and knowledgeable citizenry can compel the proper meshing of the huge industrial and military machinery of defense with our peaceful methods and goals, so that security and liberty may prosper together.

en: One test of how well a president speaks is how long the speech lives. Here we are 50 years later, we're still talking about this speech.
ko: 대통령 연설의 좋은 척도 중 하나는 얼마나 오래 살아남느냐입니다. 50년이 지난 지금도 이 연설을 이야기하고 있습니다.
ja: One test of how well a president speaks is how long the speech lives. Here we are 50 years later, we're still talking about this speech.
zhTW: One test of how well a president speaks is how long the speech lives. Here we are 50 years later, we're still talking about this speech.
vi: One test of how well a president speaks is how long the speech lives. Here we are 50 years later, we're still talking about this speech.

en: Now on Friday noon, I am to become a private citizen. I am proud to do so.
ko: 금요일 정오에 저는 일반 시민이 됩니다. 자랑스럽게 생각합니다.
ja: Now on Friday noon, I am to become a private citizen. I am proud to do so.
zhTW: Now on Friday noon, I am to become a private citizen. I am proud to do so.
vi: Now on Friday noon, I am to become a private citizen. I am proud to do so.

en: I look forward to it. Thank you, and good night.
ko: 기대하고 있습니다. 감사합니다, 안녕히 주무세요.
ja: I look forward to it. Thank you, and good night.
zhTW: I look forward to it. Thank you, and good night.
vi: I look forward to it. Thank you, and good night.
